title: Dear Grif
content:
"Dear Grif,
    Here is a whiff
    Of beautiful spring flowers;
    The big red rose
    Is for your nose,
    As toward the sky it towers.


    "Oh, do not frown
    Upon this crown
    Of green pinks and blue geranium
    But think of me
    When this you see,
    And put it on your cranium."
meaning:
Louisa May Alcott's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Dear Grif"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
